rows.description: Computus Gu: Paynter Rectory a secundo die
rows.notes: Top-most line of the page title; clear handwriting and no monetary columns present.
Second line of the page title/dating; clearly a header with no amounts.

rows.description: Imprimis reddit comput: de remanentib: in cista
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings clearly aligned. Pence looks like '4' with a small trailing loop that might be a halfpenny mark; chosen plain 4d because the fraction mark is faint and ambiguous.
Clear three-column numbers '60 0 0' aligned with ruling lines; unambiguous.
All three figures (76, 13, 4) are legible and well aligned to the vertical £ / s / d lines.
Pounds '20' and '0 0' for s and d are clear and aligned. Description legible.
Pounds '30' and zeros visible. The place-name spelling is slightly uncertain (double n), but the amounts are clear.

Description includes a small marginal note 'pro mi' (interpreted as 'pro mi:'). Numeric group reads as '03 00 8' but the pounds value is comparatively small and the handwriting is slightly shakier here; alignment to the columns is moderately clear.
Figures '02 15 7' are clearly visible and aligned. Description is clear.
The place-name appears as 'Chacchill' but there is no obvious numerical entry on the same line; the dashed rule continues across the page. Amount column at this row appears blank or omitted.
Numbers read as '03 8 11' (pence 11 is clearly a two-stroke '11'). Alignment consistent with £/s/d lines; ink slightly heavier but digits legible.
Figures '04 0 0' clearly visible and aligned; description 'Tree quitrents' legible.
Figures '01 12 0' are clear; description handwriting is legible ("Ralph marshally capons").
Reads '00 10 0' clearly (10 shillings). Description is clear.
Reads '00 15 0' clearly (15 shillings). Description is legible.

There is a numeric group '04 0 0' below Bampton in the numeric column; exact textual alignment with 'Mountague' is moderately uncertain because of spacing. Chose to attribute 04 0 0 to 'Mountague' since subsequent rows align with smaller sums.

A '01 12 0' group appears in the vertical sequence; due to tight spacing among Mountague / Hugheys / Greenesay lines the exact mapping is uncertain. Assigned 01 12 0 here; the digits themselves are legible.
A '00 10 0' appears in the vertical numeric list; mapping to Greenesay is somewhat ambiguous because of cramped lines. The numbers themselves are clear.
Next numeric group in the column reads '00 15 0' which I have attributed to 'Walker' though the page spacing makes the exact row-to-number assignment moderately uncertain.
No clear number on the same horizontal line for 'Minne'; appears to be left blank in the numeric columns or very faint. Description is legible.

There is a two-part numeric group around here read as '02 5 5' with a small trailing mark after the pence that resembles a farthing symbol (q). Interpreted pence fraction as 0.25 (one farthing). The alignment to 'Leaden-hall' is moderately uncertain because of cramped text, but the numeric pattern is present.
Numeric group '01 18 7' is clear and sits next to the bracketed pair on the left (Tintinhull block). Attribution to Molineux is likely due to vertical spacing; digits are legible.

The entry is a bracketed pair of names 'Tintinhull { Napper / Hopkins }' with two numeric lines following; the bracket indicates two split amounts on the next two lines rather than a single amount on this descriptive line itself.
Under the bracket 'Tintinhull', the first sub-line (Napper) appears to have '02 5 9' (pence '9' is slightly cramped). The bracketed layout implies these are sub-amounts for the two names.
Second sub-line under Tintinhull clearly shows '01 18 7'. Handwriting and alignment good here.
Clear numeric group '03 19 8' aligned with the line for 'Clifton Ferry'.
Numeric group '01 15 6' is visible and well aligned with 'Bensington'.

There is a visible '01 15 6' around this area as well; cramped spacing between Bensington / Brewhouse / Trethewin makes precise assignment between these three lines moderately uncertain. The digits themselves are readable.
The numeric group '06 9 7' is clear and appears on the last set of itemized lines before the final total; matched to 'Trelhewin' (spelling varies) by vertical position.

Final descriptive line 'Merton Vicar.' present with no clear numeric entry immediately to its right; may be a non-monetary note or omitted amount. There is some faint ink below but not legible as a number.

Final boxed/underlined total at bottom-right reads clearly as '230 13 8' and is used as alignment reference for the £ / s / d column lines. Text preceding it (abbreviated Latin) is legible enough to identify as a total.
